title: rosa rugosa subsp. coruscans
intro: Rosa rugosa subsp. coruscans, also known as the rugosa rose, is a shrub-like plant with fragrant flowers and edible hips, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicylic acid and other flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa rugosa subsp. coruscans include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support digestive health. Rosa rugosa subsp. coruscans can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, and salves, with the hips being used as a natural source of vitamin C and other nutrients.
